description: The Event class handles data storage, serialization, string interpolation, and memory estimation.
reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because the class encapsulates multiple unrelated responsibilities: it acts as a data carrier (ConvertedMap), handles complex I/O concerns (JSON/CBOR serialization in toJson/fromJson), implements business logic (tagging/timestamp parsing), and provides utility functions like string interpolation (sprintf) and resource management (estimateMemory).

description: AbstractPipelineExt manages pipeline execution, plugin lifecycle, instrumentation, and external resource integration.
reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because it centralizes too many distinct concerns. It manages the pipeline execution state, acts as a coordinator for plugin instantiation (PluginFactory), handles instrumentation and flow metric calculations (initializeFlowMetrics), and manages external resources like the Dead Letter Queue (dlqWriter) and Secret Stores.

description: The Queue class manages high-level queue logic, low-level page I/O, and data deserialization.
reasoning: God Class smell -> SRP violation because it mixes high-level queue management (head/tail pages, unread counts) with low-level infrastructure concerns (disk space validation in ensureDiskAvailable, file locking) and data transformation logic (reflection-based deserialization in the deserialize method).

description: DeadLetterQueueWriter manages both the writing of data and the enforcement of retention/storage policies.
reasoning: Divergent Change smell -> SRP violation because the class has multiple reasons to change: changes to the underlying storage format/writing mechanism, and changes to the administrative policies (age-based retention or size-based storage limits). These policies should be decoupled from the writer implementation.

description: FieldReference combines data representation with complex string parsing logic.
reasoning: Large Class smell -> SRP violation because it encapsulates both the identity of a field path (the data structure) and the complex logic required to parse that path from a string (StrictTokenizer). The parsing logic is a separate responsibility from the representation of the parsed result.
